retentionTimePeriodTooltip: How long to keep backups? Make sure you have enough storage space.
retentionCountTooltip: Number of most recent backups to keep. Older backups will be automatically deleted.
gfsDescription: GFS (Grandfather-Father-Son) is a backup rotation scheme that keeps backups at different intervals: hourly, daily, weekly, monthly, and yearly.
cronExpressionHelp: Use standard cron format (e.g., 0 2 * * * for daily at 2 AM)
enableCompressionDescription: Compress backup files to save space
deleteBackupConfirmation: Are you sure you want to delete this backup? This action cannot be undone.
backupSizeLimitDescription: Maximum size for a single backup (in MB)
downloadBackupFilePostgres: Download backup file. It can be restored manually via pg_restore (from custom format)
downloadBackupFileMysql: Download backup file. It can be restored manually via mysql client (from SQL dump)
downloadBackupFileMariadb: Download backup file. It can be restored manually via mariadb client (from SQL dump)
downloadBackupFileMongodb: Download backup file. It can be restored manually via mongorestore (from archive)
fileSizeDescription: The file size we actually store in storage (local, S3, Google Drive, etc.), usually compressed in ~5x times
scheduledBackupsDisabled: Scheduled backups are disabled (you can enable it back in backup configuration)
cronFormat: Cron format: minute hour day month weekday (UTC)
encryptionTooltip: If backup is encrypted, backup files in your storage (S3, local, etc.) cannot be used directly. You can restore backups through Databasus or download them unencrypted via 'Download' button.
storePeriodTooltip: How long to keep backups? Make sure you have enough storage space.
retryBackupIfFailedTooltip: Automatically retry failed backups. Backups can fail due to network failures, storage issues or temporary database unavailability.
maxFailedTriesCountTooltip: Maximum number of retry attempts for failed backups. You will receive a notification when all tries have failed.
maxFailedTriesTooltip: Maximum number of retry attempts for failed backups
maxBackupSizeLimitTooltip: Limits size of each individual backup. Note that backups are typically 15× smaller than database size. For example, a 100 MB backup represents approximately 1.5 GB database.
maxBackupSizeTooltip: Limits size of each individual backup
limitTotalBackupsSizeTooltip: Limits total size of all backups in storage (like S3, local, etc.). Once this limit is exceeded, the oldest backups are automatically removed until total size is within limit again.
storageDescription: Storage - is a place where backups will be stored (local disk, S3, Google Drive, etc.)
changeStorageWarning: If you change the storage, all backups in this storage will be deleted.
